title: [Q] What's the status to support Web Stream ?
author: AlwaysNoobCoder
body:
Web Stream are supported in modern browsers.

Do we have plan to support it ?  Are there technical obstacles to implement?

see: #1228, after four years it's still open, no response from team members.

comments.author: danielegarciav
comments.body: Might just have to create a fork that uses the Web Stream API instead of Node.js streams.

comments.author: lionel-rowe
markedwards
comments.body: @lionel-rowe there is a [Writable.fromWeb()](https://nodejs.org/api/stream.html#streamwritablefromwebwritablestream-options) method that takes care of this, actually.  It's marked as experimental, but it seems to work well.

comments.author: lionel-rowe
comments.body:
> [@lionel-rowe](https://github.com/lionel-rowe) there is a [Writable.fromWeb()](https://nodejs.org/api/stream.html#streamwritablefromwebwritablestream-options) method that takes care of this, actually. It's marked as experimental, but it seems to work well.

Yeah I saw that, but I'd rather not make NodeJS writable streams into a runtime dependency when only a small fraction of their logic is used.

comments.author: markedwards
comments.body: Isn't Node.js `Stream` already a runtime dependency of ExcelJS?

comments.author: lionel-rowe
comments.body:
> Isn't Node.js Stream already a runtime dependency of ExcelJS?

I think (?) readable is but not writable, unless writable gets pulled in somewhere indirectly.
